word.english: well-mannered, to have good manners (from adab)
word to denote a future action (see Lesson 15)
to defend; to rear, breed (from bela)
to jot down, note down (from catat)
a collective prefix, indicating a great number of people and not always translated eg. para pembaca (our readers), para tetamu (all guests), para pelajar (students in general); (para-para) shelf
to switch on; to attach (from pasang)
to rear, breed, to look after
(word of possession eg. dia punya) his
(word of possession eg. kepunyaan saya) mine
to hold eg. a meeting (from ada)
to pour into a mould (from acu)
(from pantau) to monitor, keep a tab on
to play truant, absent from school or work without permission
(from ponteng) absent from school or work without permission
paragraph, clause; matter, affair; cause, reason
legal ruling according to Islamic law
to give a legal ruling according to Islamic law

a prefix indicating in or at a place
(di hadapan) in front of
(di belakang) behind

(telefon rumah) house telephone
(telefon pejabat) office telephone
(telefon bimbit) handphone

a tuberous root
(ubi kentang) potato
(ubi kayu) tapioca
(meninggal dunia or just meninggal) pass away, die

room
(bilik mandi) bathroom
(bilik tidur) bedroom
(bilik makan) dining room

boy or girl
(budak lelaki) boy
(budak perempuan) girl

strike (beat, hit)
(pukul dua, etc.) two o'clock
to eat; (makan pagi) breakfast; (makan tengah hari) lunch; (makan malam) dinner

(1) (exclamation) Help!
(2) please - asking a service or favour of someone (see Lesson 38)
word that indicates the present continuous tense (see Lesson 31)
word that indicates the past tense (see Lesson 35)
word that indicates the past tense (see Lesson 35)

trunk, stem (of tree)
numerical coefficient (see Lesson 60)
(berpacaran/berpacar-pacaran dengan) to have a love affair with/to love each other)
prohibited by Islam from marrying (as of close relations)
